role_title: Staff Product Manager I, Ad Serving

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with adtech platform focus

level_signals: Staff PM I title signals senior IC level above Senior PM
Comp range $176K-$220K base consistent with Staff-level IC in NYC at Lyft
Expected to set team goals, roadmap, and lead engineers and data scientists

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of product management with a record of launching and scaling ad platforms from 0 to 1.
a record of launching and scaling ad platforms from 0 to 1.

Deep expertise in either real-time bidding or ad serving domains, with experience working on one or more of the following: ad decisioning logic, targeting systems, scoring, ranking, advertiser optimization (e.g. impressions, clicks, engagements, conversions), yield optimization, integrations with SSPs and DSPs.
Build a roadmap and lead a core team of engineers and data scientists to achieve it.
Align cross functional partners and teams beyond your immediate development team to drive a shared vision. For example, Sales, Legal, Finance, Operations, and internal marketing teams.
Take a holistic yield management approach across direct demand and external supply channels.
Clearly communicate priorities, problems, experiments, and decisions across a wide spectrum of audiences, from partner teams to executives.
Completely own your team's products, existing or new, from ideation through development, launch, growth, and maintenance.
Be able to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of products.
Experience leading post-launch optimizations, including developing testing strategies.
Natural ability to make things happen around you. You manage project ambiguity, complexity and interdependencies in an organized and structured way.
Strong communication skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level — within teams, within the company, to users, to press, and other audiences.
Ability to conceptualize, manage and prioritize multiple projects for stakeholders.
Detail oriented with superior organizational skills — balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation.

scope.surface: Ad Serving platform powering delivery of ads to riders before, during, and after rides within Lyft's mobility advertising network
scope.business_model: B2B2C advertising network; brand advertisers targeting riders via Lyft's platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Yield optimization across direct and programmatic demand
Ad platform scale and revenue growth
core_responsibilities: Develop strategy and objectives for the Ad Serving platform and build roadmap
Lead a core team of engineers and data scientists
Align cross-functional partners across Sales, Legal, Finance, Operations, and marketing teams
Take a holistic yield management approach across direct demand and external supply channels
Communicate priorities, problems, experiments, and decisions to partner teams and executives
Own team's products from ideation through development, launch, growth, and maintenance
Set team goals, success metrics, and roadmap aligned with Lyft's mission
Serve as champion of advertisers and advocate for their needs at all levels

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range clearly disclosed ($176K-$220K base) plus equity and bonus
Well-defined product surface with clear ownership (Ad Serving platform)
Growing business unit (Lyft Ads described as rapidly growing)
Hybrid schedule with specific days called out (Mondays, Wednesdays, Thursdays) — predictable
Up to 4 weeks per year work-from-anywhere flexibility stated
Generous parental leave (18 weeks) and comprehensive benefits described
red_flags: Role requires in-office presence in NYC 3 days/week — eliminates remote candidates
Very specific domain requirement (RTB or ad serving expertise) makes this a narrow search
Visa sponsorship status not addressed anywhere in the JD
8+ years minimum is a hard bar with no stated flexibility or 'equivalent experience' language

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff PM I', comp range, and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and domain context; not listed explicitly as KPIs in JD)
ambiguities: 'Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation' is listed under the 'Experience' section alongside hard requirements but reads as a cultural/motivational qualifier; classified as nice-to-have soft.
Bonus is mentioned as excluded from the base range ('not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits') but no bonus range or structure is provided.
The JD does not clarify whether the Staff PM I will formally manage people or only lead cross-functional influence — classified as manages_people: false based on language used.
